sentence_reviews.text: Fairness and subgroup analysis measure performance, treatment, representation, and error disparities across protected or operationally meaningful groups.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF covers fairness, harmful bias, representativeness, validity, measurement, and impact across groups and contexts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Broadly supported by NIST fairness and bias governance concepts, but the exact list is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis is to connect Statistics & experimental design, Causal inference to downstream capabilities including Medical imaging & clinical decision support, Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports fairness/bias measurement and governance; AIMA supports statistical and causal reasoning foundations for AI systems.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible relationship, but downstream examples are atlas graph terminology.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Medical imaging & clinical decision support
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF treats fairness and bias as governance concerns for AI systems in consequential domains.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible domain synthesis; not directly verified for medical imaging by the packet source.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports bias and fairness analysis for AI decision-support contexts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible and well aligned with fairness evaluation, but phrased as graph taxonomy.

sentence_reviews.text: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Statistics & experimental design, Causal inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF emphasizes contextual, socio-technical risk management and measurement limits; AIMA supports statistical/causal foundations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Measurement limitations are plausible, but the adversary wording is inherited boilerplate and less specific to fairness.

sentence_reviews.text: Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Statistics & experimental design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Fairness and subgroup disparity evaluation require measurement, sampling, metrics, and statistical comparison; NIST and AIMA support these foundations.

sentence_reviews.text: Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Causal inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA covers causal reasoning; NIST AI RMF supports bias/fairness analysis where causal factors and impacts may matter.
sentence_reviews.notes: Causal inference is a defensible core dependency for many fairness analyses, though not every fairness metric is causal.

sentence_reviews.text: Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Human evaluation & preference studies.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports human-centered evaluation, stakeholder impacts, and governance processes for fairness and bias risks.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common dependency is plausible, but not directly established as necessary.

edge_reviews.notes: Statistical measurement is core to subgroup disparity analysis.
Causal reasoning is important for fairness analysis, though the core strength may depend on the fairness method.
Human evaluation is a reasonable common governance dependency for fairness and bias review.
